heading: Naval Observatory: exchange of information with foreign offices
label: 8716 Naval Observatory: exchange of information with foreign offices
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)7396.5 U.S.C. 464, (2d par.).Aug. 22, 1912, ch. 33537 Stat. 342In subsection (a) the words “as he may from time to time deem desirable with a view” are omitted as surplusage. The words “a larger amount of data useful” are substituted for the words “increase the total data which may be of use” for clarity.In subsection (b) the words “during the continuance of any such arrangement” are omitted as surplusage. The third proviso of is omitted as obsolete.5 U.S.C. 464

children.label: a
children.text: The Secretary of the Navy may arrange to exchange data with foreign almanac offices to reduce the duplication of work in preparing the different national nautical and astronomical almanacs and make available for publication a larger amount of data useful to navigators and astronomers. Each such arrangement shall be made terminable on one year’s notice.

children.label: b
children.text: The work of the Nautical Almanac Office shall be so conducted that in an emergency the part of the work intended for the use of navigators may be computed by the force of the office without foreign cooperation.
